若一王子神社(にゃくいちおうじじんじゃ)は、鹿児島県曽於市末吉町にある神社である。無社格。 [[ファイル:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|サムネイル|若一王子神社の社殿]] {{神社|名称=若一王子神社|所在地=[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷1589|祭神=[[天照大神]]|創建=万寿3年(1026年)12月29日|社格=無社格|別名=若一神社|地図=Japan Kagoshima|緯度度=31|緯度分=38|緯度秒=49.793|経度度=131|経度分=02|経度秒=34.865}}
== 若一王子神社について == 若一王子神社とは、紀州の[[熊野三山]](三山は昔修験道の一大行道場であった所)で、その地に鎮座されている。
旧[[官幣大社]][[熊野速玉大社|熊野速玉神社]][[祭神]]([[伊弉諾尊]])、旧[[官幣中社]][[熊野那智大社|熊野那智神社]]祭神([[伊邪那美命]])の御子である天照大神をこの地から[[勧請]]されたものと言われている。
全国には若宮王子、若王子、若宮若女一王子、若宮一王子、若一王子などととなえ、奉祀されている神社が多く散在している。若宮とは、本宮の御子神の意「王子」とは[[仏語 (曖昧さ回避)|仏語]]の[[童子]]にあたるが、ここでは「御子神」の意味に用いてあり、熊野五所王子の内第一との意味である。よって、若一王子は熊野三所大神に次ぐ座に位置する神であるとある。
== 若一王子神社の創建 == [[薩摩国|薩]][[大隅国|隅]]地に[[班田収授法|班田収授]]が行われたのは、[[延暦]]19年([[800年]])12月であったが、せっかくの班田制も結実せず、また旧に還って[[墾田]]状態が久しく続いた。[[後一条天皇]]の[[万寿]]3年([[1026年]])正月20日[[大宰府]]の大監[[平季基|平朝臣季基]]により弟[[判官]]良宗と共に現在の[[都城市]]の本庄、三俣院に至り、同地を領し近郷一円に[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]]の拡大を図った。
同年9月9日現在の梅北に守護神として[[神柱宮]]を創建し、自ら司官となったが、その後荘園を[[藤原頼通]]に献上し、その管理を婿の肝付兼貞に譲って箸野に隠居し、同年12月29日当社を建て、晩年を司官として本明屋敷にて余生を送った。その後に祠があり、木造が祭ってあり近くには墓がある。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> ファイル:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|若一王子神社の鳥居(2015年3月8日撮影) </gallery>
== 関連項目 ==
* [[島津荘]] * [[神柱宮]] * [[黒尾神社]] * [[肝付氏]]
[[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:曽於市]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:曽於市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{神社| |画像=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
社殿 |名称=若一王子神社 |所在地=[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷1589 |祭神=[[天照大神]] |創建=万寿3年(1026年)12月29日 |社格=無社格 |別名=若一神社 |地図=Japan Kagoshima |緯度度=31|緯度分=38|緯度秒=49.793 |経度度=131|経度分=02|経度秒=34.865 }} '''若一王子神社'''（にゃくいちおうじじんじゃ）は、[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷にある神社である{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}。無社格。 == 若一王子について == 若一王子神社とは、[[紀伊国|紀州]]の[[熊野三山]]（三山は昔修験道の一大行道場であった所）に由来する神社である。 旧[[官幣大社]][[熊野速玉大社|熊野速玉神社]][[祭神]]（[[伊弉諾尊]]）、旧[[官幣中社]][[熊野那智大社|熊野那智神社]]祭神（[[伊邪那美命]]）の御子である天照大神をこの地から[[勧請]]されたものと言われている。 全国には若宮王子、若王子、若宮若女一王子、若宮一王子、若一王子などととなえ、奉祀されている神社が多く散在している。若宮とは、本宮の御子神の意「王子」とは[[仏語 (曖昧さ回避)|仏語]]の[[童子]]にあたるが、ここでは「御子神」の意味に用いてあり、熊野五所王子の内第一との意味である。よって、[[若一王子]]は熊野三所大神に次ぐ座に位置する神であるとある。 == 若一王子神社の創建 == [[薩摩国|薩]][[大隅国|隅]]地に[[班田収授法|班田収授]]が行われたのは、[[延暦]]19年（[[800年]]）12月であったが、せっかくの班田制も結実せず、また旧に還って[[墾田]]状態が久しく続いた。[[後一条天皇]]の[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）正月20日[[大宰府]]の大監[[平季基|平朝臣季基]]により弟[[判官]]良宗と共に現在の[[都城市]]の本庄、三俣院に至り、同地を領し近郷一円に[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]]の拡大を図り、後に日本最大の荘園となる[[島津荘]]の原型を作った。 同年9月9日、梅北（現：都城市[[梅北町]]）の地に守護神として[[神柱宮]]を創建し、自ら司官となったが、その後荘園を[[藤原頼通]]に献上し、その管理を婿の肝付兼貞に譲って箸野に隠居し、同年12月29日当社を建て、晩年を司官として本明屋敷にて余生を送った。その後に祠があり、木造が祭ってあり近くには墓がある。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|若一王子神社の鳥居(2015年3月8日撮影) == 関連項目 == * [[島津荘]] * [[神柱宮]] * [[黒尾神社]] * [[肝付氏]] == 外部リンク == *https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ {{Authority control}} == 参考資料 == === 出典 === [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:曽於市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] {{DEFAULTSORT:にやくいちおうししんしや}}

revision.text:
{{Original research|date=June 2021}} {{Shrine |Image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Name=若一王子神社 |Location=[[Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[Soo City]], [[Sueyoshi Town]] Minamigō 1589 |Deity=[[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |Founded=Manju 3 (1026), December 29 |Rank=None |Alias=若一神社 |Map=Japan Kagoshima |LatDeg=31|LatMin=38|LatSec=49.793 |LonDeg=131|LonMin=02|LonSec=34.865 }} '''Wakaichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Minamigō, [[Sueyoshi Town]], [[Soo City]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]{{Cite web|和書|title=Wakaichi Ōji Shrine {{!}} Soo Navi｜Official Website of Soo City Tourism Association|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=June 26, 2021}}. It has no shrine rank. == About Wakaichi Ōji == The Wakaichi Ōji Shrine originates from the {{ill|Three Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山}} in {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}. These shrines were once a major center of ascetic practices in the Shugendō tradition. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, considered the child of the deities {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊}} of the former {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社}} {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊邪那美命}} of the former {{ill|Kanpei Chūsha|ja|官幣中社}} {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}}, who was brought ({{ill|kanjō|ja|勧請}}) to this region. Shrines dedicated to deities referred to as Wakamiyawakaōji, Wakamiyaichiōji, or Wakaichi Ōji are scattered across Japan. "Wakamiya" signifies the child deity of the main shrine ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}), while "Ōji" relates to the Buddhist term for {{ill|Dōji|ja|童子}}. Here, it is used to mean "child deity," specifically referring to the primary deity of the {{ill|Kumano Five Shrines|ja|熊野五所王子}}. Consequently, Wakaichi Ōji is considered second only to the deities of the Three Kumano Shrines. == Founding of Wakaichi Ōji Shrine == The {{ill|Handen Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} was implemented in the [[Satsuma Province|Satsu]]{{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}} region in Enryaku 19 (800), December. However, the system was short-lived, and land ownership returned to the {{ill|Konden|ja|墾田}} system for an extended period. In the third year of {{ill|Manju|ja|万寿}} (1026), on January 20, {{ill|Taira no Sukimoto|ja|平季基}} of the {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} administration, along with his brother Ryōsō, arrived at Honjō and Mimitsu-in in the present {{ill|Miyakonojo|ja|都城市}} area. They expanded nearby estates, laying the foundation of what would later become the largest estate in Japan, {{ill|Shimazu-shō|ja|島津荘}}. On September 9 of the same year, they established {{ill|Kashihara-no-miya|ja|神柱宮}} in the Umekita area (present-day Miyakonojo's Umekita Town) as a guardian deity. Afterward, the estate was presented to {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}, and its management was entrusted to his son-in-law, Kimotsuki Kanesada. Sukimoto retired to Hashino, built this shrine on December 29, and spent his final years in the Honmeya residence. A wooden shrine was erected later, and the site includes Sukimoto's grave. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate of Wakaichi Ōji Shrine (photo taken on March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu-shō|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kashihara-no-miya|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuro-o Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Wakaichi Ōji Shrine on Kagoshima Shrine Association website] == References == === Sources === {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano Faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}}

Sukimoto retired to Hashino, built this shrine on December 29, and spent his final years in the Honmeya residence. A wooden shrine was erected later, and the site includes Sukimoto's grave. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate of Wakaichi Ōji Shrine (photo taken on March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu-shō|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kashihara-no-miya|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuro-o Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Wakaichi Ōji Shrine on Kagoshima Shrine Association website] == References == === Sources === {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano Faith]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}}

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]][[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]

{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Shrine Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location=[[Soo City|曽於市]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture|鹿児島県]] | address=末吉町南之郷1589 | deity=[[Amaterasu]] | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. ## About Niyakuichi Ōji Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine is said to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, shrines dedicated to "Wakamiya Ōji" and variations like "Wakaichihime Ōji" exist, honoring similar divine connections. ## Creation of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine The shrine was established during the Manju era when land reform efforts in the region were under {{ill|Heian-period|ja|平安時代}} governance. Local developments led to the foundation of {{ill|Shimozuma Manor|ja|島津荘}}, which became Japan's largest estate. In December 1026, the shrine was built to serve as a protective deity for the region, influenced by nearby developments like {{ill|Kashiwara Shrine|ja|神柱宮}}. ## Gallery File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photographed March 8, 2015) ## Related Topics * {{ill|Shimozuma Manor|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kashiwara Shrine|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} ## References {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Shrine Official Information] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]][[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]

{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Shrine Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kashiwara Shrine|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kashiwara Shrine|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]][[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]

{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Shrine Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]][[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]

Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]

{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Shrine Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]][[en:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyakuichi ōji-jinja (So City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]

{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Shrine Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Shrine Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * {{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市|12=draft}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|12=draft}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=draft}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦|12=draft}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿|12=draft}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=draft}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|12=draft}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=draft}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:c]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: c]] [[Category:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)}}

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|12=draft}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=draft}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦|12=draft}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿|12=draft}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=draft}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|12=draft}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=draft}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)}}

In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿|12=draft|12=draft}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=draft|12=draft}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=draft|12=draft}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)}}

revision.comment: 15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市) up to rev 98258518
revision.text:
{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{神社| |画像=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
社殿 |名称=若一王子神社 |所在地=[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷1589 |祭神=[[天照大神]] |創建=万寿3年(1026年)12月29日 |社格=無社格 |別名=若一神社 |地図=Japan Kagoshima |緯度度=31|緯度分=38|緯度秒=49.793 |経度度=131|経度分=02|経度秒=34.865 }} '''若一王子神社'''（にゃくいちおうじじんじゃ）は、[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷にある神社である{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}。無社格。 == 若一王子について == 若一王子神社とは、[[紀伊国|紀州]]の[[熊野三山]]（三山は昔修験道の一大行道場であった所）に由来する神社である。 旧[[官幣大社]][[熊野速玉大社|熊野速玉神社]][[祭神]]（[[伊弉諾尊]]）、旧[[官幣中社]][[熊野那智大社|熊野那智神社]]祭神（[[伊邪那美命]]）の御子である天照大神をこの地から[[勧請]]されたものと言われている。 全国には若宮王子、若王子、若宮若女一王子、若宮一王子、若一王子などととなえ、奉祀されている神社が多く散在している。若宮とは、本宮の御子神の意「王子」とは[[仏語 (曖昧さ回避)|仏語]]の[[童子]]にあたるが、ここでは「御子神」の意味に用いてあり、熊野五所王子の内第一との意味である。よって、[[若一王子]]は熊野三所大神に次ぐ座に位置する神であるとある。 == 若一王子神社の創建 == [[薩摩国|薩]][[大隅国|隅]]地に[[班田収授法|班田収授]]が行われたのは、[[延暦]]19年（[[800年]]）12月であったが、せっかくの班田制も結実せず、また旧に還って[[墾田]]状態が久しく続いた。[[後一条天皇]]の[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）正月20日[[大宰府]]の大監[[平季基|平朝臣季基]]により弟[[判官]]良宗と共に現在の[[都城市]]の本庄、三俣院に至り、同地を領し近郷一円に[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]]の拡大を図り、後に日本最大の荘園となる[[島津荘]]の原型を作った。 同年9月9日、梅北（現：都城市[[梅北町]]）の地に守護神として[[神柱宮]]を創建し、自ら司官となったが、その後荘園を[[藤原頼通]]に献上し、その管理を婿の肝付兼貞に譲って箸野に隠居し、同年12月29日当社を建て、晩年を司官として本明屋敷にて余生を送った。その後に祠があり、木造が祭ってあり近くには墓がある。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|若一王子神社の鳥居(2015年3月8日撮影) == 関連項目 == * [[島津荘]] * [[神柱宮]] * [[黒尾神社]] * [[肝付氏]] == 外部リンク == *https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ {{Authority control}} == 参考資料 == === 出典 === [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:曽於市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] {{DEFAULTSORT:にやくいちおうししんしや}}

{{独自研究|date=2021年6月}} {{神社| |画像=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
社殿 |名称=若一王子神社 |所在地=[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷1589 |祭神=[[天照大神]] |創建=万寿3年(1026年)12月29日 |社格=無社格 |別名=若一神社 |地図=Japan Kagoshima |緯度度=31|緯度分=38|緯度秒=49.793 |経度度=131|経度分=02|経度秒=34.865 }} '''若一王子神社'''（にゃくいちおうじじんじゃ）は、[[鹿児島県]][[曽於市]][[末吉町]]南之郷にある神社である{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}。無社格。 == 若一王子について == 若一王子神社とは、[[紀伊国|紀州]]の[[熊野三山]]（三山は昔修験道の一大行道場であった所）に由来する神社である。 旧[[官幣大社]][[熊野速玉大社|熊野速玉神社]][[祭神]]（[[伊弉諾尊]]）、旧[[官幣中社]][[熊野那智大社|熊野那智神社]]祭神（[[伊邪那美命]]）の御子である天照大神をこの地から[[勧請]]されたものと言われている。 全国には若宮王子、若王子、若宮若女一王子、若宮一王子、若一王子などととなえ、奉祀されている神社が多く散在している。若宮とは、本宮の御子神の意「王子」とは[[仏語 (曖昧さ回避)|仏語]]の[[童子]]にあたるが、ここでは「御子神」の意味に用いてあり、熊野五所王子の内第一との意味である。よって、[[若一王子]]は熊野三所大神に次ぐ座に位置する神であるとある。 == 若一王子神社の創建 == [[薩摩国|薩]][[大隅国|隅]]地に[[班田収授法|班田収授]]が行われたのは、[[延暦]]19年（[[800年]]）12月であったが、せっかくの班田制も結実せず、また旧に還って[[墾田]]状態が久しく続いた。[[後一条天皇]]の[[万寿]]3年（[[1026年]]）正月20日[[大宰府]]の大監[[平季基|平朝臣季基]]により弟[[判官]]良宗と共に現在の[[都城市]]の本庄、三俣院に至り、同地を領し近郷一円に[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]]の拡大を図り、後に日本最大の荘園となる[[島津荘]]の原型を作った。 同年9月9日、梅北（現：都城市[[梅北町]]）の地に守護神として[[神柱宮]]を創建し、自ら司官となったが、その後荘園を[[藤原頼通]]に献上し、その管理を婿の肝付兼貞に譲って箸野に隠居し、同年12月29日当社を建て、晩年を司官として本明屋敷にて余生を送った。その後に祠があり、木造が祭ってあり近くには墓がある。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|若一王子神社の鳥居(2015年3月8日撮影) == 関連項目 == * [[島津荘]] * [[神柱宮]] * [[黒尾神社]] * [[肝付氏]] == 外部リンク == *https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ {{Authority control}} == 参考資料 == === 出典 === [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:曽於市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] {{DEFAULTSORT:にやくいちおうししんしや}}

{{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Soo City|ja|曽於市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official Rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|ja|延暦}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju 3 (1026 CE)|ja|万寿}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)}} {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]]
