Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|ja|島津荘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Estate|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]]

Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location=[[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official Rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]]. The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|qq=}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]]

Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo, Kagoshima]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location=[[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official Rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]]. The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|qq=}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:bbb]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[en:Nyakuichi ōji-]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo, Kagoshima]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Soo City]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo, Kagoshima]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Soo City
revision.text:
{{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=[[File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg|250px]]
Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location=[[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official Rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]]. The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|qq=}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo, Kagoshima]] [[Category:曽於市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Soo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

Shrine hall | name=若一王子神社 | location=[[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 }} '''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}} The shrine holds no official Rank. == About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]]. The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine. == Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|qq=}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates. On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate. == Gallery == File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) == See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information] == References == {{Reflist}} [[ja (So City)]] {{translated page|ja|若一王子神社 (曽於市)|version=98258518|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōji]] }} [[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] [[ja:若一王子神社]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Soo, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Soo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]].
The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|qq=}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}

'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]].
The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|qq=|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|qq=|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information]

'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]].
The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information]

'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]].
The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information]

'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]].
The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q107410083]] Wikidata name: Nyakuichi ōji-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg | name=若一王子神社 | location=[[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 | prefecture={{ill|Sō|WD=Q858382}} | coordinates={{Coord|31.647165|N|131.043018|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Soo, Kagoshima|Soo City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]].
The shrine enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku 19 (800 CE)]], but these reforms ultimately failed. In [[:en:Manju (era)|Manju 3 (1026 CE)]], Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]], one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * [[:en:Shimazu Estate|Shimazu Estate]] * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若一王子神社 (曽於市)]]
{{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Soo, Kagoshima|en|Soo, Kagoshima|WD=Q858382|lt=Soo City}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 | prefecture={{ill|Sō|WD=Q858382}} | coordinates={{Coord|31.647165|N|131.043018|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Soo, Kagoshima|en|Soo, Kagoshima|WD=Q858382|lt=Soo City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}}.
The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|en|Kumano Nachi Taisha|WD=Q710359|lt=Kumano Nachi Taisha}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku 19 (800 CE)}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju (era)|en|Manju (era)|WD=Q1194155|lt=Manju 3 (1026 CE)}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-oosumi/%E6%9B%BD%E6%96%BC%E5%B8%82/1316/ Official Shrine Information]

== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate|1=Shimazu Estate}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}

{{独自研究|date=2021st year 6月}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Infobox | image=File:若一王子神社の社殿.jpg | name=若一王子神社 | location={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Soo, Kagoshima|en|Soo, Kagoshima|WD=Q858382|lt=Soo City|1=Soo, Kagoshima}}, Sueyoshi Town, Minamigō 1589 | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} | established=December 29, 1026 (Manju 3) | status=None | alias=若一神社 | map=Japan Kagoshima | lat_deg=31|lat_min=38|lat_sec=49.793 | lon_deg=131|lon_min=02|lon_sec=34.865 | prefecture={{ill|Sō|WD=Q858382|1=Soo, Kagoshima}} | coordinates={{Coord|31.647165|N|131.043018|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine''' (若一王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Soo, Kagoshima|en|Soo, Kagoshima|WD=Q858382|lt=Soo City|1=Soo, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=若一王子神社 {{!}} そおナビ｜鹿児島県曽於市観光協会公式サイト|url=https://soo-navi.jp/spot/2839/|website=soo-navi.jp|accessdate=2021-06-26}}</ref> The shrine holds no official Rank.
== About Niyakuichi Ōji == Niyakuichi Ōji shrines are linked to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a major spiritual site associated with {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}.
The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, the offspring of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, deities of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} and {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|en|Kumano Nachi Taisha|WD=Q710359|lt=Kumano Nachi Taisha|1=Kumano Nachi Taisha}} respectively. Across Japan, many shrines with variations like "Wakamiya Ōji" and "Niyakuichi Ōji" exist, all referring to child deities of the main shrine.
== Establishment of Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine == Land reforms under the {{ill|Handen-Shūju Law|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju Law|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} began in the region during {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku 19 (800 CE)|1=Enryaku}}, but these reforms ultimately failed. In {{ill|Manju (era)|en|Manju (era)|WD=Q1194155|lt=Manju 3 (1026 CE)|1=Manju (era)}}, Daikan {{ill|Taira no Sukemoto|ja|平季基|1=Taira no Suemoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Sukemoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Suemoto|qq=}} oversaw the creation of {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate|1=Shimazu Estate}}, one of Japan's largest feudal estates.
On September 9, 1026, the deity of {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} was established as a guardian. Later in December, the Niyakuichi Ōji Shrine was constructed, symbolizing the spiritual protection for the estate.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:若一王子神社の鳥居.jpg|Torii gate (photo from March 8, 2015) </gallery>== See Also == * {{ill|Shimazu Estate|en|Shimazu Estate|WD=Q11476819|lt=Shimazu Estate|1=Shimazu Estate}} * {{ill|Kanbashira-gū|ja|神柱宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbashira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuroo Shrine|ja|黒尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroo Shrine|1=Kuroo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107410082}} * {{ill|Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimotsuki clan|lt=Kimutake Clan|ja|肝付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimutake Clan|qq=}}
